name: cattrs

metadata.name: cattrs
metadata.summary: Composable complex class support for attrs and dataclasses.

documentation: # cattrs --- **cattrs** is an open source Python library for structuring and unstructuring data. _cattrs_ works best with _attrs_ classes, dataclasses and the usual Python collections, but other kinds of classes are supported by manually registering converters. Python has a rich set of powerful, easy to use, built-in data types like dictionaries, lists and tuples. These data types are also the lingua franca of most data serialization libraries, for formats like json, msgpack, cbor, yaml or toml. Data types like this, and mappings like `dict` s in particular, represent unstructured data. Your data is, in all likelihood, structured: not all combinations of field names or values are valid inputs to your programs. In Python, structured data is better represented with classes and enumerations. _attrs_ is an excellent library for declaratively describing the structure of your data, and validating it. When you're handed unstructured data (by your network, file system, database...), _cattrs_ helps to convert this data into structured data. When you have to convert your structured data into data types other libraries can handle, _cattrs_ turns your classes and enumerations into dictionaries, integers and strings. Here's a simple taste. The list containing a float, an int and a string gets converted into a tuple of three ints. ```python >>> import cattrs >>> cattrs.structure([1.0, 2, "3"], tuple[int, int, int]) (1, 2, 3) ``` _cattrs_ works well with _attrs_ classes out of the box. ```python >>> from attrs import frozen >>> import cattrs >>> @frozen # It works with non-frozen classes too. ... class C: ... a: int ... b: str >>> instance = C(1, 'a') >>> cattrs.unstructure(instance) {'a': 1, 'b': 'a'} >>> cattrs.structure({'a': 1, 'b': 'a'}, C) C(a=1, b='a') ``` Here's a much more complex example, involving `attrs` classes with type metadata. ```python >>> from enum import unique, Enum >>> from typing import Optional, Sequence, Union >>> from cattrs import structure, unstructure >>> from attrs import define, field >>> @unique ... class CatBreed(Enum): ... SIAMESE = "siamese" ... MAINE_COON = "maine_coon" ... SACRED_BIRMAN = "birman" >>> @define ... class Cat: ... breed: CatBreed ... names: Sequence[str] >>> @define ... class DogMicrochip: ... chip_id = field() # Type annotations are optional, but recommended ... time_chipped: float = field() >>> @define ... class Dog: ... cuteness: int ... chip: Optional[DogMicrochip] = None >>> p = unstructure([Dog(cuteness=1, chip=DogMicrochip(chip_id=1, time_chipped=10.0)), ... Cat(breed=CatBreed.MAINE_COON, names=('Fluffly', 'Fluffer'))]) >>> print(p) [{'cuteness': 1, 'chip': {'chip_id': 1, 'time_chipped': 10.0}}, {'breed': 'maine_coon', 'names': ('Fluffly', 'Fluffer')}] >>> print(structure(p, list[Union[Dog, Cat]])) [Dog(cuteness=1, chip=DogMicrochip(chip_id=1, time_chipped=10.0)), Cat(breed=, names=['Fluffly', 'Fluffer'])] ``` Consider unstructured data a low-level representation that needs to be converted to structured data to be handled, and use `structure`. When you're done, `unstructure` the data to its unstructured form and pass it along to another library or module. Use [attrs type metadata](http://attrs.readthedocs.io/en/stable/examples.html#types) to add type metadata to attributes, so _cattrs_ will know how to structure and destructure them. - Free software: MIT license - Documentation: https://catt.rs - Python versions supported: 3.8 and up. (Older Python versions are supported by older versions; see the changelog.) ## Features - Converts structured data into unstructured data, recursively: - _attrs_ classes and dataclasses are converted into dictionaries in a way similar to `attrs.asdict`, or into tuples in a way similar to `attrs.astuple`. - Enumeration instances are converted to their values. - Other types are let through without conversion. This includes types such as integers, dictionaries, lists and instances of non-_attrs_ classes. - Custom converters for any type can be registered using `register_unstructure_hook`. - Converts unstructured data into structured data, recursively, according to your specification given as a type. The following types are supported: - `typing.Optional[T]`. - `typing.List[T]`, `typing.MutableSequence[T]`, `typing.Sequence[T]` (converts to a list). - `typing.Tuple` (both variants, `Tuple[T, ...]` and `Tuple[X, Y, Z]`). - `typing.MutableSet[T]`, `typing.Set[T]` (converts to a set). - `typing.FrozenSet[T]` (converts to a frozenset). - `typing.Dict[K, V]`, `typing.MutableMapping[K, V]`, `typing.Mapping[K, V]` (converts to a dict). - `typing.TypedDict`. - _attrs_ classes with simple attributes and the usual `__init__`. - Simple attributes are attributes that can be assigned unstructured data, like numbers, strings, and collections of unstructured data. - All _attrs_ classes and dataclasses with the usual `__init__`, if their complex attributes have type metadata. - `typing.Union` s of supported _attrs_ classes, given that all of the classes have a unique field. - `typing.Union` s of anything, given that you provide a disambiguation function for it. - Custom converters for any type can be registered using `register_structure_hook`. _cattrs_ comes with preconfigured converters for a number of serialization libraries, including json, msgpack, cbor2, bson, yaml and toml. For details, see the [cattrs.preconf package](https://catt.rs/en/stable/preconf.html). ## Design Decisions _cattrs_ is based on a few fundamental design decisions. - Un/structuring rules are separate from the models. This allows models to have a one-to-many relationship with un/structuring rules, and to create un/structuring rules for models which you do not own and you cannot change. (_cattrs_ can be configured to use un/structuring rules from models using the [`use_class_methods` strategy](https://catt.rs/en/latest/strategies.html#using-class-specific-structure-and-unstructure-methods).) - Invent as little as possible; reuse existing ordinary Python instead. For example, _cattrs_ did not have a custom exception type to group exceptions until the sanctioned Python [`exceptiongroups`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ExceptionGroup). A side-effect of this design decision is that, in a lot of cases, when you're solving _cattrs_ problems you're actually learning Python instead of learning _cattrs_. - Refuse the temptation to guess. If there are two ways of solving a problem, _cattrs_ should refuse to guess and let the user configure it themselves. A foolish consistency is the hobgoblin of little minds so these decisions can and are sometimes broken, but they have proven to be a good foundation. ## Additional documentation and talks - [On structured and unstructured data, or the case for cattrs](https://threeofwands.com/on-structured-and-unstructured-data-or-the-case-for-cattrs/) - [Why I use attrs instead of pydantic](https://threeofwands.com/why-i-use-attrs-instead-of-pydantic/) - [cattrs I: un/structuring speed](https://threeofwands.com/why-cattrs-is-so-fast/) - [Python has a macro language - it's Python (PyCon IT 2022)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UYRSixikUTo) - [Intro to cattrs 23.1](https://threeofwands.com/intro-to-cattrs-23-1-0/) ## Credits Major credits to Hynek Schlawack for creating [attrs](https://attrs.org) and its predecessor, [characteristic](https://github.com/hynek/characteristic). _cattrs_ is tested with [Hypothesis](http://hypothesis.readthedocs.io/en/latest/), by David R. MacIver. _cattrs_ is benchmarked using [perf](https://github.com/haypo/perf) and [pytest-benchmark](https://pytest-benchmark.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html). This package was created with [Cookiecutter](https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter) and the [`audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage`](https://github.com/audreyr/cookiecutter-pypackage) project template.

functions.name: cattrs.v.format_exception
documentation:
The default exception formatter, handling the most common exceptions.

The following exceptions are handled specially:

* `KeyErrors` (`required field missing`)
* `ValueErrors` (`invalid value for type, expected <type>` or just `invalid value`)
* `TypeErrors` (`invalid value for type, expected <type>` and a couple special
  cases for iterables)
* `cattrs.ForbiddenExtraKeysError`
* some `AttributeErrors` (special cased for structing mappings)

functions.name: cattrs.v.transform_error
documentation:
Transform an exception into a list of error messages.

To get detailed error messages, the exception should be produced by a converter
with `detailed_validation` set.

By default, the error messages are in the form of `{description} @ {path}`.

While traversing the exception and subexceptions, the path is formed:

- by appending `.{field_name}` for fields in classes
- by appending `[{int}]` for indices in iterables, like lists
- by appending `[{str}]` for keys in mappings, like dictionaries

```
param exc

The exception to transform into error messages.

param path

The root path to use.

param format_exception

A callable to use to transform  into
string descriptions of errors.
```

:::{versionadded} 23.1.0
:::

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch._DispatchNotFound
documentation: A dummy object to help signify a dispatch not found.

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch.FunctionDispatch

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.FunctionDispatch.dispatch
documentation: Return the appropriate handler for the object passed.

FunctionDispatch is similar to functools.singledispatch, but
instead dispatches based on functions that take the type of the
first argument in the method, and return True or False.

objects that help determine dispatch should be instantiated objects.

classes.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.register_cls_list
documentation: Register a class to direct or singledispatch.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.register_func_list
documentation:
Register a predicate function to determine if the handle
should be used for the type.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.clear_direct
documentation: Clear the direct dispatch.

methods.name: cattrs.dispatch.MultiStrategyDispatch.clear_cache
documentation: Clear all caches.

MultiStrategyDispatch uses a combination of exact-match dispatch,
singledispatch, and FunctionDispatch.

```
param fallback_factory

A hook factory to be called when a hook cannot be
produced.
```

:::{versionchanged} 23.2.0 Fallbacks are now factories.
:::

modules.name: cattrs.strategies._class_methods
documentation: Strategy for using class-specific (un)structuring methods.

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._class_methods.use_class_methods
documentation:
Configure the converter such that dedicated methods are used for (un)structuring
the instance of a class if such methods are available. The default (un)structuring
will be applied if such an (un)structuring methods cannot be found.

```
param converter

The  on which this strategy is applied. You can use
 or any other derived class.

param structure_method_name

Optional string with the name of the class method
which should be used for structuring. If not provided, no class method will be
used for structuring.

param unstructure_method_name

Optional string with the name of the class method
which should be used for unstructuring. If not provided, no class method will
be used for unstructuring.
```

If you want to (un)structured nested objects, just append a converter parameter
to your (un)structuring methods and you will receive the converter there.

:::{versionadded} 23.2.0
:::

functions.name: cattrs.gen._shared.find_structure_handler
documentation:
Find the appropriate structure handler to use.

Return `None` if no handler should be used.

modules.name: cattrs.strategies._subclasses
documentation: Strategies for customizing subclass behaviors.

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._subclasses._has_subclasses
documentation: Whether the given class has subclasses from `given_subclasses`.

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._subclasses.include_subclasses
documentation:
Configure the converter so that the attrs/dataclass `cl` is un/structured as if it
was a union of itself and all its subclasses that are defined at the time when this
strategy is applied.

```
param cl

A base  or  class.

param converter

The  on which this strategy is applied. Do note that
the strategy does not work for a .

param subclasses

A tuple of sublcasses whose ancestor is . If left as ,
subclasses are detected using recursively the  method of 
and its descendents.

param union_strategy

A callable of two arguments passed by position
(, ) that defines the union strategy to use to
disambiguate the subclasses union. If  (the default), the automatic unique
field disambiguation is used which means that every single subclass
participating in the union must have an attribute name that does not exist in
any other sibling class.

param overrides

a mapping of  attribute names to overrides (instantiated with
) to customize un/structuring.
```

:::{versionadded} 23.1.0
:::

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._subclasses._include_subclasses_with_union_strategy
documentation:
This function is tricky because we're dealing with what is essentially a circular
reference.

We need to generate a structure hook for a class that is both:
* specific for that particular class and its own fields
* but should handle specific functions for all its descendants too

Hence the dance with registering below.

class_variables.name: cattrs.gen._consts.AttributeOverride.omit
documentation: Omit the field completely.

class_variables.name: cattrs.gen._consts.AttributeOverride.struct_hook
documentation: Structure hook to use.

class_variables.name: cattrs.gen._consts.AttributeOverride.unstruct_hook
documentation: Structure hook to use.

modules.name: cattrs.gen._lc
documentation: Line-cache functionality.

functions.name: cattrs.gen._lc.generate_unique_filename
documentation:
Create a "filename" suitable for a function being generated.

If *lines* are provided, insert them in the first free spot or stop
if a duplicate is found.

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.msgpack
documentation: Preconfigured converters for msgpack.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.msgpack.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the msgpack library.

* datetimes are serialized as timestamp floats
* sets are serialized as lists

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._unions.default_tag_generator
documentation: Return the class name.

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._unions.configure_tagged_union
documentation:
Configure the converter so that `union` (which should be a union) is
un/structured with the help of an additional piece of data in the
unstructured payload, the tag.

```
param converter

The converter to apply the strategy to.

param tag_generator

A  function is used to map each
member of the union to a tag, which is then included in the
unstructured payload. The default tag generator returns the name of
the class.

param tag_name

The key under which the tag will be set in the
unstructured payload. By default, .

param default

An optional class to be used if the tag information
is not present when structuring.
```

The tagged union strategy currently only works with the dict
un/structuring base strategy.

:::{versionadded} 23.1.0
:::

functions.name: cattrs.strategies._unions.configure_union_passthrough
documentation:
Configure the converter to support validating and passing through unions of the
provided types and their subsets.

For example, all mature JSON libraries natively support producing unions of ints,
floats, Nones, and strings. Using this strategy, a converter can be configured
to efficiently validate and pass through unions containing these types.

The most important point is that another library (in this example the JSON
library) handles producing the union, and the converter is configured to just
validate it.

Literals of provided types are also supported, and are checked by value.

NewTypes of provided types are also supported.

The strategy is designed to be O(1) in execution time, and independent of the
ordering of types in the union.

If the union contains a class and one or more of its subclasses, the subclasses
will also be included when validating the superclass.

:::{versionadded} 23.2.0
:::

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.ujson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for ujson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.ujson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the ujson library.

* bytes are serialized as base64 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* sets are serialized as lists

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.tomlkit
documentation: Preconfigured converters for tomlkit.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.tomlkit.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the tomlkit library.

* bytes are serialized as base85 strings
* sets are serialized as lists
* tuples are serializas as lists
* mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.pyyaml
documentation: Preconfigured converters for pyyaml.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.pyyaml.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the pyyaml library.

* frozensets are serialized as lists
* string enums are converted into strings explicitly
* datetimes and dates are validated

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.cbor2
documentation: Preconfigured converters for cbor2.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.cbor2.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the cbor2 library.

* datetimes are serialized as timestamp floats
* sets are serialized as lists

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.json
documentation: Preconfigured converters for the stdlib json.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.json.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the stdlib json module.

* bytes are serialized as base64 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* counters are serialized as dicts
* sets are serialized as lists

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.bson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for bson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.bson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the bson library.

* sets are serialized as lists
* byte mapping keys are base85-encoded into strings when unstructuring, and reverse
* non-string, non-byte mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring
* a deserialization hook is registered for bson.ObjectId by default

classes.name: cattrs.preconf.bson.Base85Bytes
documentation: A subclass to help with binary key encoding/decoding.

modules.name: cattrs.preconf.orjson
documentation: Preconfigured converters for orjson.

functions.name: cattrs.preconf.orjson.configure_converter
documentation:
Configure the converter for use with the orjson library.

* bytes are serialized as base85 strings
* datetimes are serialized as ISO 8601
* sets are serialized as lists
* string enum mapping keys have special handling
* mapping keys are coerced into strings when unstructuring

functions.name: cattrs.fns.raise_error

classes.name: cattrs.errors.StructureHandlerNotFoundError

Error raised when structuring cannot find a handler for converting inputs into
{attr}`type_`.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.IterableValidationNote

class_variables.name: cattrs.errors.IterableValidationNote.index
documentation: Ints for list indices, strs for dict keys
Attached as a note to an exception when an iterable element fails structuring.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.IterableValidationError

methods.name: cattrs.errors.IterableValidationError.group_exceptions
documentation: Split the exceptions into two groups: with and without validation notes.
Raised when structuring an iterable.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.AttributeValidationNote
documentation: Attached as a note to an exception when an attribute fails structuring.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.ClassValidationError

methods.name: cattrs.errors.ClassValidationError.group_exceptions
documentation: Split the exceptions into two groups: with and without validation notes.
Raised when validating a class if any attributes are invalid.

classes.name: cattrs.errors.ForbiddenExtraKeysError
documentation:
Raised when `forbid_extra_keys` is activated and such extra keys are detected
during structuring.

The attribute `extra_fields` is a sequence of those extra keys, which were the
cause of this error, and `cl` is the class which was structured with those extra
keys.

modules.name: cattrs.disambiguators
documentation: Utilities for union (sum type) disambiguation.

functions.name: cattrs.disambiguators.is_supported_union
documentation: Whether the type is a union of attrs classes.

functions.name: cattrs.disambiguators.create_default_dis_func
documentation:
Given attrs classes, generate a disambiguation function.

The function is based on unique fields or unique values.

```
param use_literals

Whether to try using fields annotated as literals for
disambiguation.
```

functions.name: cattrs.gen.typeddicts.make_dict_unstructure_fn
documentation:
Generate a specialized dict unstructuring function for a TypedDict.

```
param cl

A  class.

param converter

A Converter instance to use for unstructuring nested fields.

param kwargs

A mapping of field names to an , for
customization.

param _cattrs_detailed_validation

Whether to store the generated code in the
_linecache_, for easier debugging and better stack traces.
```

functions.name: cattrs.gen.typeddicts.make_dict_structure_fn
documentation:
Generate a specialized dict structuring function for typed dicts.

```
param cl

A  class.

param converter

A Converter instance to use for structuring nested fields.

param kwargs

A mapping of field names to an , for
customization.

param _cattrs_detailed_validation

Whether to use a slower mode that produces
more detailed errors.

param _cattrs_forbid_extra_keys

Whether the structuring function should raise a
 if unknown keys are encountered.

param _cattrs_detailed_validation

Whether to store the generated code in the
_linecache_, for easier debugging and better stack traces.
```

:::{versionchanged} 23.2.0 The `_cattrs_forbid_extra_keys` and `_cattrs_detailed_validation` parameters take their values from the given converter by default.
:::

If true, _attrs_ fields marked as

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstruct_strat
documentation: The default way of unstructuring `attrs` classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class.

The converter function should take an instance of the class and return
its Python equivalent.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook_func
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class, using
a function to check if it's a match.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_unstructure_hook_factory
documentation:
Register a hook factory for a given predicate.

```
param predicate

A function that, given a type, returns whether the factory
can produce a hook for that type.

param factory

A callable that, given a type, produces an unstructuring
hook for that type. This unstructuring hook will be cached.
```

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook
documentation:
Register a primitive-to-class converter function for a type.

The converter function should take two arguments:
  * a Python object to be converted,
  * the type to convert to

and return the instance of the class. The type may seem redundant, but
is sometimes needed (for example, when dealing with generic classes).

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook_func
documentation:
Register a class-to-primitive converter function for a class, using
a function to check if it's a match.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.register_structure_hook_factory
documentation:
Register a hook factory for a given predicate.

```
param predicate

A function that, given a type, returns whether the factory
can produce a hook for that type.

param factory

A callable that, given a type, produces a structuring
hook for that type. This structuring hook will be cached.
```

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure
documentation: Convert unstructured Python data structures to structured data.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstructure_attrs_asdict
documentation: Our version of `attrs.asdict`, so we can call back to us.
Classes to Python primitives.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.unstructure_attrs_astuple
documentation: Our version of `attrs.astuple`, so we can call back to us.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_enum
documentation: Convert an enum to its value.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_seq
documentation: Convert a sequence to primitive equivalents.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_mapping
documentation: Convert a mapping of attr classes to primitive equivalents.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._unstructure_union
documentation:
Unstructure an object as a union.

By default, just unstructures the instance.
note: Use UnionType when 3.11 is released as
the behaviour of @final is changed. This would
affect how we can support UnionType in ._compat.py

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._gen_structure_generic
documentation: Create and return a hook for structuring generics.
Python primitives to classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._gen_attrs_union_structure
documentation:
Generate a structuring function for a union of attrs classes (and maybe None).

```
param use_literals

Whether to consider literal fields.
```

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_call
documentation:
Just call `cl` with the given `obj`.

This is just an optimization on the `_structure_default` case, when
we know we can skip the `if` s. Use for `str`, `bytes`, `enum`,
etc.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure_attrs_fromtuple
documentation: Load an attrs class from a sequence (tuple).
Attrs classes.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_attribute
documentation: Handle an individual attrs attribute.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.structure_attrs_fromdict
documentation: Instantiate an attrs class from a mapping (dict).

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_list
documentation: Convert an iterable to a potentially generic list.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_deque
documentation: Convert an iterable to a potentially generic deque.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_set
documentation: Convert an iterable into a potentially generic set.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_frozenset
documentation: Convert an iterable into a potentially generic frozenset.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_dict
documentation: Convert a mapping into a potentially generic dict.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_union
documentation: Deal with structuring a union.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._structure_tuple
documentation: Deal with structuring into a tuple.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter._get_dis_func
documentation: Fetch or try creating a disambiguation function for a union.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.BaseConverter.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of the converter, keeping all existing custom hooks.

```
param detailed_validation

Whether to use a slightly slower mode for detailed
validation errors.
```
Converts between structured and unstructured data.

classes.name: cattrs.converters.Converter

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.__init__
documentation:
---
param detailed_validation: |-
  Whether to use a slightly slower mode for detailed
  validation errors.
param structure_fallback_factory: |-
  A hook factory to be called when no
  registered structuring hooks match.
param unstructure_fallback_factory: |-
  A hook factory to be called when no
  registered unstructuring hooks match.
---

:::{versionadded} 23.2.0 *unstructure_fallback_factory*
:::

:::{versionadded} 23.2.0 *structure_fallback_factory*
:::

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.gen_structure_annotated
documentation: A hook factory for annotated types.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.gen_unstructure_typeddict
documentation:
Generate a TypedDict unstructure function.

Also apply converter-scored modifications.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.gen_unstructure_optional
documentation: Generate an unstructuring hook for optional types.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.gen_structure_typeddict
documentation:
Generate a TypedDict structure function.

Also apply converter-scored modifications.

methods.name: cattrs.converters.Converter.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of the converter, keeping all existing custom hooks.

param detailed_validation

Whether to use a slightly slower mode for detailed
validation errors.

A converter which generates specialized un/structuring functions.
